name: Question Type Taxonomy — The Six Question Types of Long-Onboarding
definition_origin: Empirical pattern formalized via Mobbin teardowns and Cal-AI / Flo / Noom / Headway public funnel analysis (2023-2026). No single canonical originator; derived from convergent practice. Reforge curriculum (Casey Winters), Maja Voje's go-to-market work, and Adapty's 'High-Performing Onboarding 2026' all reference variants of the taxonomy. The 'identity > demographic' insight is closest to Susan Weinschenk's identity-based design (2011) and BJ Fogg's identity-based habits (Stanford Behavior Design Lab).
core_mechanism: Questions are not data collectors — they are emotional pacing devices. A homogeneous string of demographic questions feels like a survey (which the user came to escape), so engagement decays. Mixing question types creates a narrative arc: low-friction openers establish flow state, preference questions establish psychological ownership (IKEA effect), painpoint questions activate loss aversion, identity questions trigger self-perception theory (Bem 1972 — 'I am the kind of person who...'), and social-proof punctuation closes the credibility gap. Each type pulls a different cognitive lever, so the user never adapts to one register and disengages.
neuroscience_correlate: Identity questions engage the medial prefrontal cortex (self-referential processing, Kelley et al. 2002). Painpoint questions activate the anterior insula (loss anticipation). Preference questions engage ventromedial prefrontal cortex (subjective value coding). The variety itself recruits novelty-detection circuits (locus coeruleus / VTA) — homogeneous question runs let those systems habituate, predicting fatigue.
empirical_evidence: Mobbin teardown of Cal AI (33 steps): six distinct question types interleaved — preference (color/style), demographic (age/sex/height/weight), goal (target weight, weekly pace), painpoint (what doesn't work today), identity (active/sedentary/disciplined), and social-proof punctuation ('5M users like you have hit their goal'). Adapty 2026 onboarding teardown: top-decile apps average 4-6 question types in their flow; bottom-decile apps use 1-2 (typically demographic-only). Headway, Flo, Noom, Headspace, Prayer Lock all converge on the same mix. Self-perception theory replication is robust: Aronson & Mettee 1968, Bem 1972, Cialdini's commitment-and-consistency literature (~50 years of replication). No public peer-reviewed RCT compares question-type-mix specifically; evidence is strong industry pattern + foundational psychology.
measurable_outcome_metric: Onboarding completion rate, step-by-step abandonment rate, time-on-step distribution, paywall reach rate, trial-start rate post-paywall, D1/D7 retention conditioned on completion. Per-step-type abandonment heatmap.
evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + practitioner_opinion (foundational psychology peer-reviewed; specific taxonomy is industry pattern)
conversion_lift_benchmark: Adapty 2026: long-onboarding (>15 steps) with 4+ question types reaches paywall at ~70-80% vs ~35-50% for demographic-only flows [agency benchmark, not RCT]. Cal AI's mixed-type 33-step flow reportedly converts to trial at >25% (vs category mean ~10-15%) per public teardowns.
engineering_tactics: Define each type as a typed component in your onboarding state machine. TypeScript example: `type QuestionStep = | { kind: 'preference'; id: string; options: PreferenceOption[]; }; | { kind: 'demographic'; id: string; field: 'age' | 'gender' | 'height' | 'weight' | 'birthdate'; }; | { kind: 'goal'; id: string; goalType: 'numeric' | 'enum'; }; | { kind: 'painpoint'; id: string; prompt: string; choices: string[]; allowOther?: boolean; }; | { kind: 'identity'; id: string; prompt: string; choices: IdentityChoice[]; }; | { kind: 'social_proof'; id: string; metric: string; cohort_phrase: string; };`. Renderer dispatches to a typed component per kind. (1) Preference questions FIRST — pick a color, pick an avatar, pick a tone; cost is zero, ownership begins. (2) Demographic NEVER FIRST and never clustered — break them up; pair each with a justification ('We use this to calibrate your plan, not to identify you'). (3) Goals MIDDLE — anchor the value of the rest of the flow. (4) Painpoint placement = engineered peak of negative emotion (insula activation) — must be IMMEDIATELY followed by a reassurance frame ('You're not alone — here's how we handle this'). (5) Identity questions late — by step ~70-80%, user is invested enough to declare. (6) Social-proof punctuation between every 4-6 functional steps — full-bleed card with testimonial/cohort stat. Identity question phrasing: 'Are you a runner?' (identity) outperforms 'Do you run?' (behavior). Avoid open-ended text inputs except for one optional field — they tank completion.
real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI step 3: preference — 'Pick your goal' (large emoji/illustration buttons: lose weight / build muscle / maintain). Step 7: painpoint — 'What's hardest about your current routine?' multi-select. Step 12: identity — 'How would you describe yourself?' (active / occasionally active / sedentary, each with a body silhouette illustration). Step 15: social-proof — 'You're in good company — 5M+ people are tracking with us.' Step 22: AI plan reveal — peak. (2) Flo period tracker: cycle preference (color theme) → demographic (birthdate, country) → goal (track period / get pregnant / track contraception) → painpoint ('Which symptoms bother you most?' multi-select with custom emoji). (3) Noom: 30+ step quiz mixing preference (eating triggers), demographic (age/weight/height), goal (target weight), painpoint ('When do you eat when not hungry?'), identity ('I am the kind of person who...'). (4) Headway book-summary app: preference (genres) → goal (read more / learn faster / sleep better) → identity ('Lifelong learner / casual reader / re-engaging') → social-proof ('80% of users finish their first book in 7 days'). (5) Prayer Lock (Mau Baron): preference (faith tradition) → painpoint ('What's hardest about your prayer practice?') → identity ('Are you a daily / occasional / returning believer?') → AI prayer reveal.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary; single question per screen with full-thumb-zone tap targets is the dominant pattern. Web allows multi-question per page (column layout) for mandatory KYC but mirrors mobile single-question for marketing onboarding. Mobile must avoid keyboard-heavy text inputs (drop-off triples on text questions vs tap questions); web is more forgiving but still benefits from tap-only.
platform_design_language_alignment: Aligned with both Liquid Glass and M3 Expressive — both prize large tap targets and expressive type. Liquid Glass's bolder left-aligned alert typography fits the conversational question header; M3 Expressive's spring motion fits the answer-selected micro-celebration. Avoid system-default checkbox/radio components — they read as form, not journey.
category_fit: Strongest for wellness/habit, AI-wrapper, fitness, mental-health, fintech-personalization. Weak for utility apps with universal value (calculators, weather), productivity B2B (use empty-state seeding instead), and games (intro tutorial replaces questionnaire).
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. Build the typed schema + 6 component types in 2-3 days; content authoring + copy iteration takes the rest. Server-driven UI (item 31) extends this to weeks but unlocks zero-deploy iteration.
tooling_options: OnboardJS (open-source onboarding state machine), Plotline (server-driven onboarding for mobile), Appcues Mobile, Pendo Mobile, Product Fruits. For AI-personalization layer: Adapty Onboarding Builder (2026), Superwall onboarding paywalls, native React Native + XState v5 (item 30).
ai_dependency: none for the taxonomy itself. Optional for AI-stitching answers across steps (item 14) which uses a small LLM call (gpt-4o-mini class) at the AI plan reveal step (item 12).
claude_design_application: Instruct Claude with the typed schema explicitly. Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate 6 React Native onboarding screen components, one per QuestionStep kind (preference, demographic, goal, painpoint, identity, social_proof). Each screen renders in a state-machine context (type Context = { answers: Record<string, unknown>; userArchetype: ArchetypeKey; locale: string; }). Forbid: a single generic <SurveyQuestion> component that renders all kinds. Each kind must have its own visual treatment: preference = large illustration tiles, demographic = compact form input, goal = numeric stepper or enum tiles, painpoint = multi-select with empathetic header, identity = single-select with self-image illustrations, social_proof = full-bleed testimonial card. Use Reanimated 3 for the answer-selected micro-celebration; springConfig {damping:14, stiffness:180}. Forbid Inter; use Cabinet Grotesk for headers, Söhne for body.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Author the question-content JSON for 12 mixed-type steps for a Magician-archetype habit app, alternating types so no two same-type questions are consecutive. Painpoint must come at step 6 of 12 (engineered low-point), social-proof at step 8 (recovery), identity at step 10 (commitment).'
stitch_application: Stitch: spec must describe each question type as a distinct visual frame. Prompt fragment 1: 'Mockup 6 onboarding frames for a wellness app, one per question type. Frame 1 (preference): single question header in 28px Cabinet Grotesk weight 350, four large 152x152px illustration tiles in 2x2 grid, warm cream background #F4EFE6, ember accent on selection #E36B2C. Frame 2 (demographic): compact birthdate picker, justification microcopy below in Söhne 12px italic. Frame 3 (goal): three vertical cards with outcome illustration + label. Frame 4 (painpoint): empathetic header (How can we help?), six multi-select pills with subtle border, allow-other text-input at bottom. Frame 5 (identity): three full-bleed photo-illustration tiles with overlay caption (I am a... morning person / night owl / unpredictable). Frame 6 (social-proof): full-bleed quote card with testimonial photo, pull-quote 32px Cabinet Grotesk italic, attribution below, NO logo cloud. Forbid: Lucide icons, indigo gradient, emoji bullets, generic Inter.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to a single homogeneous <SurveyQuestion> component that renders all questions identically as a label + radio-group + Continue button — DMV-form aesthetic. Counter-prompt: 'There are six question types and each must look visibly different. Output a typed schema FIRST as a TypeScript discriminated union, then implement six distinct components. Forbid a single generic component. Forbid radio-group HTML element — use custom-styled tappable cards. Forbid the word survey in copy.' AI also defaults to clustering demographic questions consecutively (age → gender → height → weight) which kills flow; counter: 'No two questions of the same kind may be consecutive. Output the step sequence as an explicit array with kind annotations and verify the no-consecutive rule before generating components.'
anti_patterns: (1) Cluster of demographic questions at the start — feels like a tax form, drives 30-50% drop-off in first 3 steps. (2) Identity questions framed as behavior (Do you exercise? vs Are you an athlete?) — weaker self-perception trigger. (3) Painpoint without recovery — eliciting negative emotion and not following with reassurance is dark-pattern adjacent (DFA flag). (4) Open-ended text questions — mobile keyboard kills flow; tank completion ~3x. (5) Single-type onboarding (all preference, all demographic) — bores the brain. (6) Social-proof copy that's clearly fabricated — 5M users when you have 12 invites lawsuit risk + slop tell.
regulatory_risk: Medium. GDPR data-minimization (Art. 5(1)(c)) — every demographic question must justify collection. CCPA also requires purpose-bound collection. COPPA 2026: birthdate gating must be neutral (Are you 13+? is non-compliant); use a date picker that accepts a real birthdate and routes accordingly. EU Digital Fairness Act draft Q4 2026 flags pseudo-personalization as manipulative. UK AADC: identity questions for under-18 users must default to high-privacy. False social-proof statistics = FTC § 5 deceptive-practice exposure.
platform_constraints_2026: iOS App Store 5.1.1(v) data minimization — onboarding cannot demand data unrelated to functionality. Apple's App Privacy Report and Privacy Nutrition Label must accurately reflect what's collected. Google Play Data Safety form must mirror. Apple guidance discourages requiring sensitive personal info before functionality demonstration.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 — every tap target ≥ 44x44 pt (2.5.8 24x24 css min). VoiceOver/TalkBack: each illustration tile needs explicit accessible label, not just emoji. Dynamic Type support so 200% scaling doesn't truncate questions. Identity questions with body-silhouette illustrations need text alternatives that don't reveal sensitive content (avoid 'overweight male silhouette' as alt text — say 'Body type: A'). prefers-reduced-motion: replace selection-spring with crossfade.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) Long-onboarding fatigue is real — the 33-step ceiling is empirical; 40+ steps tank completion. (2) Cultural variance — JP/KR users prefer fewer painpoint questions (face-saving cultures); US/EU tolerate more. (3) Latency — synchronous server roundtrip per step reads as broken; persist locally, sync async (item 32). (4) Don't reveal answer-affecting logic in real time ('You picked weight loss, so your plan will...') until the AI plan reveal moment — premature reveal kills the peak.
cross_cultural_validity: JP/KR/CN: prefer reassurance-heavy framing on painpoint questions; identity questions may feel intrusive. LATAM: warmer copy register, social-proof testimonials weight more than statistics. MENA: family-context identity questions ('How does your family fit your goals?') resonate vs Western individualist framings. RTL layouts: progress arrow and selection animation must mirror.
prescribed_action: Define your onboarding as 6 question types in a typed discriminated union. Mix types so no two same-kind questions are consecutive. Place a painpoint question at the engineered low-point (~50-60% through) immediately followed by a social-proof recovery card. Use identity-framed questions ('Are you the kind of person who...') in the final third, not behavior-framed. Forbid: clustered demographic openers, open-ended text inputs, fabricated stats.
questionnaire_relevance: Core — defines the six question types and their sequencing constraints.
aha_relevance: Indirect — types build sunk cost + identity commitment that makes the AI plan reveal land harder. Identity question right before reveal is canonical Cal-AI move.
telemetry_events: onboarding_step_viewed { step_id, kind, step_index, total_steps, ad_variant, locale }, onboarding_step_completed { step_id, kind, answer_value_hash, time_on_step_ms }, onboarding_step_abandoned { step_id, kind, time_on_step_ms }, onboarding_question_type_distribution_per_user (derived).
measurement_horizon: Day 0 paywall-reach rate; D7 retention conditioned on which question types they completed.
emotional_archetype_alignment: Caregiver: lean preference + identity, mute painpoint intensity. Magician: preference + painpoint + identity (full transformation arc). Hero: goal + painpoint + identity (challenge arc). Sage: minimize emotional questions; lean evidence and demographic with justifications. Jester: preference + playful identity questions, skip heavy painpoint. Outlaw: painpoint as anti-establishment framing ('What about the system isn't working for you?').
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'survey-style demographic dump' (homogeneous form-style) and 'open-text dump' (every question a free-text input).
sources: [Mau Baron — Prayer Lock Funnel Playbook (Starter Story)](https://www.starterstory.com/mou)
uncertain: specific RCT-grade conversion-lift figures for question-type mix
